unit_19 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_19

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 294 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC SINGLE
Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.
答案：E　a vacuous pastime
refinedelitistedifying
refined: standard unit headword | elitist: standard unit headword | edifying: standard unit headword
#2 SE
Critics of compost production often do not fully consider the potential environmental benefits of compost use - benefits that might __________ the energy consumption, gaseous emissions, and other negative environmental effects attributable to compost production.Critics of compost production often do not fully consider the potential environmental benefits of compost use - benefits that might __________ the energy consumption, gaseous emissions, and other negative environmental effects attributable to compost production.
答案：B, F　offset; counterbalance
offsetproductionemission
offset: standard unit headword | production: standard unit headword | emission: standard unit headword
#3 Sentence Equivalence
Any antimatter in our part of the universe is necessarily ______ because of the overwhelming preponderance of ordinary matter, by which antimatter is quickly annihilated.Any antimatter in our part of the universe is necessarily ______ because of the overwhelming preponderance of ordinary matter, by which antimatter is quickly annihilated.
答案：A, F　short-lived; ephemeral
preponderanceoverwhelming
preponderance: standard unit headword | overwhelming: standard unit headword
#4 Text Completion
The combination of Isabella Beeton’s short life and the ______ of evidence for some parts of her story means that at times her biographer, Kathryn Hughes, is forced to fill out her narrative with background information.The combination of Isabella Beeton’s short life and the ______ of evidence for some parts of her story means that at times her biographer, Kathryn Hughes, is forced to fill out her narrative with background information.
答案：D　paucity
forcenarrative
force: standard unit headword | narrative: standard unit headword

One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.The artist's portraits of the social elite, with their luscious sensuality and insistent prettiness, have often been regarded with sly amusement, if not outright (i) __________. One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derision; appositeness; willingness to flatter
meretriciouselite
meretricious: standard unit headword | elite: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sterling reputation of; exhaustive
sterlinggiven

The performer can be ______ in his comedy, but he is fundamentally a bighearted person who displays a core sweetness even at his most manic states.The performer can be ______ in his comedy, but he is fundamentally a bighearted person who displays a core sweetness even at his most manic states.
答案：A, F　inflammatory; provocative
puerilepedestrian
puerile: standard unit headword | pedestrian: standard unit headword
#8 SE
This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for ___________ story.This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for ___________ story.
答案：B, E　a diffuse; an amorphous
mawkishbanal
mawkish: standard unit headword | banal: standard unit headword
#9 TC TRIPLE
Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles
ecumenicalparsimonious
ecumenical: standard unit headword | parsimonious: standard unit headword
#10 Text Completion
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence
resourcefuldevastating
resourceful: standard unit headword | devastating: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　mixed; inconsistent
pedagogicaldiscouraging
pedagogical: standard unit headword | discouraging: standard unit headword

The concept of the Hellenistic period in ancient history has proved useful but also ______, with scholars disagreeing on the dates when the period began and ended.The concept of the Hellenistic period in ancient history has proved useful but also ______, with scholars disagreeing on the dates when the period began and ended.
答案：A, B　slippery; elusive
slipperyfutile
slippery: standard unit headword | futile: standard unit headword

If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve ______ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve ______ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting
endemicopposition
endemic: standard unit headword | opposition: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：C, E　repudiated; germane to

#16 Text Completion

He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　forbearance; madcap; reserved
beguilelurkedifying

#17 Text Completion
For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility
demisepiety
demise: standard unit headword | piety: standard unit headword
#18 TC DOUBLE
A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) ____________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) ____________ in the face of evidence.A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) ____________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) ____________ in the face of evidence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　distinct from; intransigent
solid
solid: standard unit headword

Therefore. a complete lack of any (i)________, human emotions in ancestral species is (ii)________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　antecedents of; improbable
antecedent

答案：E　germane to

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　noted; approbation; unqualified
approbation

Blank 2

Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay
crumble

#49 Text Completion
Despite the occasional (i)______ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)______ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)______ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)______ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)______ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)______ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from
ascetic
ascetic: standard unit headword

Blank 2

答案：A, D　preachy; sanctimonious

Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)______, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　struck by; suffocating lack of creative freedom; monitored

答案：D, F　unfounded; unjustified

答案：A　significance

答案：C, E　paltry; meager

#56 Sentence Equivalence

答案：E, F　mitigated; abated

答案：C　prestige

#63 Text Completion
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough

答案：A　jejune

Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.

(i) ___________ dams that have outlived their usefulness--or whose social and economic utility is (ii)____________ by the environmental harm they do---is an idea that is catching on. Over the past 6 years, some 175 dams have been dismantled across the United States, and more than 600 over the past century. That's just a drop in the millpond, however, given that there are perhaps 2.5 million dams in the United States and that most of those removed to date have been relatively (iii) ____________ .(i) ___________ dams that have outlived their usefulness--or whose social and economic utility is (ii)____________ by the environmental harm they do---is an idea that is catching on. Over the past 6 years, some 175 dams have been dismantled across the United States, and more than 600 over the past century. That's just a drop in the millpond, however, given that there are perhaps 2.5 million dams in the United States and that most of those removed to date have been relatively (iii) ____________ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　Demolishing; overshadowed; insignificant

答案：B　omnipresent

However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder
banal
banal: standard unit headword

答案：A　misleading

答案：B, C　circuitous; indirect

答案：A　superficial

答案：B, E　disparities; variances

#219 Text Completion

His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)______ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)______ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.During the Harlem Renaissance, Alain Locke (i)______ the first flourishing of a self-consciously racial art movement in America and was widely credited with providing the philosophical basis for its emergence. His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)______ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)______ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　presided over; undisputed; insufficient

This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.

Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unimpressed with; terrifying; a commonplace

Human brains are amazing organs but they are not perfect. Cognitive biases, as we know, sometimes produce errors. Yet the term "error" could be (i)______ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)______ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)______ .Human brains are amazing organs but they are not perfect. Cognitive biases, as we know, sometimes produce errors. Yet the term "error" could be (i)______ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)______ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　problematic; a uniquely correct; reductive
opposition

答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable to

Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate

After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii)______ , having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has setin.Acertain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i)______ .
